unpublishDialog.title: Unpublish this game?
unpublishDialog.description: Students will no longer see or play this game in the course. Your draft in Game Studio is not deleted — you can keep editing and publish again whenever you are ready.

settingsDrawer.title: Settings
settingsDrawer.themeHint: Choose the saved preview background for this project card.

ifElseDialog.title: Configure If/Else Node
ifElseDialog.description: Configure how this branch node routes players after a correct or wrong result.
ifElseDialog.deleteHint: Hold the button below for 3 seconds to delete this node.
ifElseDialog.editorAlertDescription: This node routes players to different paths based on whether the previous answer was correct or wrong. Use the messages below to explain each outcome.
ifElseDialog.overviewAlertDescription: Choose which route counts as the correct branch, then review both outgoing targets below.
ifElseDialog.fieldCorrectMessage: Message after a correct previous answer
ifElseDialog.correctMessagePlaceholder: Explain what happens when the previous answer was correct
ifElseDialog.fieldWrongMessage: Message after a wrong previous answer
ifElseDialog.wrongMessagePlaceholder: Explain what happens when the previous answer was wrong
ifElseDialog.routeSelectionHint: The blue branch node stays neutral. Orange marks the route that players follow after a correct result.
ifElseDialog.routeSelectionOptionA: Use Node A when the previous answer was correct.
ifElseDialog.routeSelectionOptionB: Use Node B when the previous answer was correct.

startDialog.title: Configure Start Node
startDialog.description: Configure the start node with title and description
startDialog.themeHint: Choose the saved preview background for this project.

endDialog.title: Configure End Node
endDialog.description: Configure the end node with title and description
endDialog.deleteHint: Hold the button below for 3 seconds to delete this node.

publishDrawer.title: Publish Game
publishDrawer.publishUnavailable: Publish is not available. Save the project first.
publishDrawer.floorNote: Wrong-answer penalties may apply; score never below 0.

publishValidation.rulesTitle: Fix these graph issues before publishing:

graphIssues.start.missing: Exactly one Start node is required.
graphIssues.start.duplicate: Only one Start node is allowed (found {{count}}).
graphIssues.start.noOutgoing: "{{label}}" must have at least one outgoing connection.
graphIssues.end.missing: Exactly one End node is required.
graphIssues.end.duplicate: Only one End node is allowed (found {{count}}).
graphIssues.game.missing: At least one game node (Image Pin, Drag & Drop Math, or Open Question) is required.
graphIssues.edge.sourceMissing: An edge references a missing source node.
graphIssues.edge.targetMissing: An edge references a missing target node.
graphIssues.edge.selfLoop: "{{label}}" has a self-loop edge, which is not allowed.
graphIssues.flow.endUnreachable: The End node is not reachable from Start.
graphIssues.ifElse.branch.missing: "{{label}}" must connect both If and Else branches.

nodeIssues.imagePin.image.missing: Image Pin "{{label}}" is missing an image.
nodeIssues.imagePin.rect.none: Image Pin "{{label}}" needs at least one target region.
nodeIssues.imagePin.rect.invalidGeometry: Image Pin "{{label}}" has a region with invalid size (region {{rectIndex}}).
nodeIssues.imagePin.rect.missingQuestion: Image Pin "{{label}}" has a region without question text (region {{rectIndex}}).
nodeIssues.imagePin.retryDeduction.invalid: Image Pin "{{label}}" has an invalid retry deduction percentage.
nodeIssues.imagePin.meta.incomplete: Image Pin "{{label}}" is missing a game description.
nodeIssues.openQuestion.prompt.missing: Open Question "{{label}}" needs at least one exercise prompt.
nodeIssues.openQuestion.answer.missing: Open Question "{{label}}" has {{count}} exercise(s) missing a reference answer.
nodeIssues.openQuestion.points.invalid: Open Question "{{label}}" must award at least 1 point.
nodeIssues.dndMath.tab.none: Drag & Drop Math "{{label}}" needs at least one exercise tab.
nodeIssues.dndMath.tab.missingTitle: Drag & Drop Math "{{label}}" has a tab without a title.
nodeIssues.dndMath.tab.emptyCanvas: Drag & Drop Math "{{label}}" tab "{{tabTitle}}" has no canvas rows.
nodeIssues.dndMath.tab.noContent: Drag & Drop Math "{{label}}" tab "{{tabTitle}}" has no content.
nodeIssues.dndMath.equation.invalid: Drag & Drop Math "{{label}}" tab "{{tabTitle}}" has an invalid equation.
nodeIssues.dndMath.reference.orphan: Drag & Drop Math "{{label}}" tab "{{tabTitle}}" has duplicate row references.
nodeIssues.dndMath.scoring.noEvaluatableRow: Drag & Drop Math "{{label}}" tab "{{tabTitle}}" has no evaluatable equation.
nodeIssues.dndMath.points.invalid: Drag & Drop Math "{{label}}" must award at least 1 point.
nodeIssues.start.meta.incomplete: Start "{{label}}" is missing a title or description.
nodeIssues.end.meta.incomplete: End "{{label}}" is missing a title or description.

previewIfElse.routingDescription: This node sends players to the next slide based on the previous result.
previewIfElse.logicSummary: This is a logic slide. It decides the next slide from the previous result and does not show normal game content.
previewIfElse.correctOutcome: If the previous answer was correct, go to {{node}}: {{destination}}.
previewIfElse.wrongOutcome: If the previous answer was wrong, go to {{node}}: {{destination}}.
previewIfElse.missingPreviousResultDescription: This logic slide cannot choose the next route until the previous slide has been checked.
previewIfElse.missingMessageTitle: Edit this logic slide to continue
previewIfElse.missingMessageDescription: Add the required branch message in the If/Else node editor before this flow can continue.

toasts.createRequiresSignin: You must be signed in to create a game

dragDropMathEditor.descriptionPlaceholder: Describe the math task, context, and what learners should do…
dragDropMathEditor.deleteExerciseTabDialogDescription: This exercise tab and all of its content — title, building blocks, and canvas — will be permanently removed. This cannot be undone.
dragDropMathEditor.sigmaWrongSourceHint: Only result chips (= value) allowed — not math or text blocks.
dragDropMathEditor.sigmaResetDialogDescription: The entire sum block will be removed from the canvas — the Sum badge, all result chips, and the calculated total. You start fresh and can drag a new Sum block from the palette.
dragDropMathEditor.canvasEmptyHint: Drag math nodes here to place them anywhere on the canvas.
dragDropMathEditor.duplicateResultAriaLabel: Drag result {{value}} into a new row to keep calculating
dragDropMathEditor.evaluateToastIncompatibleUnits: This unit combination is not allowed (e.g. m² × m² or km + kg).
dragDropMathEditor.evaluateToastParse: The expression could not be calculated.
dragDropMathEditor.evaluateToastNotFinite: The result is not a valid number.

dragDropMathGamePreview.previewNotice: Preview mode. This shows how the game looks and works while you build it.
dragDropMathGamePreview.submitAnswerPrompt: I finished my calculation on the canvas. Please check my answer.
dragDropMathGamePreview.hintPrompt: Give me a helpful hint for this exercise, but do not reveal the full answer.
dragDropMathGamePreview.howToPlayPrompt: Explain how to play this game. Tell me how to build and submit my answer using the math blocks.
dragDropMathGamePreview.howToPlayScoringResponse:
Scoring (PSCA):
1. Your answer is scored from four weighted parts: final result (50%), intermediate steps (30%), method (15%), and error-free checks (5%).
2. Step scoring uses carry mode by default: later steps are judged from your previous results, so follow-up errors are not always double-penalized.
3. Points = Score × {{maxPoints}} (up to {{maxPoints}} points for this game).
dragDropMathGamePreview.howToPlayResponse:
How to play:
1. Read the task description and the exercise title.
2. Drag Math Block, Sum, and Text Block chips from the palette onto the canvas.
3. Build your calculation row by row — press Enter on a math chip to evaluate it.
4. Drag result chips into a Sum row to add intermediate totals.
5. Use the expand control if you need more canvas space.
6. Click Submit answer when your calculation is ready.

Tip: blue feedback means the row accepted your input; red means check operators, numbers, or units.
dragDropMathGamePreview.submitConfirmDialogBody: If you submit now, your answer can no longer be changed. It will be analyzed using PSCA scoring.
dragDropMathGamePreview.maxScoreAchievedMessage: Congratulations! You achieved the maximal score 🥳

dragDropMathSettings.instantColorFeedbackHint: Blue on valid Enter, red on invalid (per row).
dragDropMathSettings.scoreAccordionHint: Scores use PSCA (Partial Scoring Carry Algorithm): four weighted components on a 0–1 scale, mapped to your maximum points.
dragDropMathSettings.scoreAccordionResultBody: Is the final answer correct within tolerance?
dragDropMathSettings.scoreAccordionStepsBody: Share of intermediate calculations that match the model. In carry mode (default), later steps use the student's upstream results instead of only the model operands — follow-up errors are not always penalized twice.
dragDropMathSettings.scoreAccordionMethodBody: Did the student choose the expected operators (×, +, Σ, etc.) for each step?
dragDropMathSettings.scoreAccordionErrorFreeBody: Units, notation, rounding, and concept checks averaged per step.
dragDropMathSettings.scoreAccordionPointsMapping: Awarded points = Score × {{maxPoints}} (example: Score 0.437 → {{examplePoints}} points at max {{maxPoints}}).
dragDropMathSettings.scoreAccordionCarryNote: Carry mode is the default for step scoring. Strict mode (optional) always compares against the model solution operands.

imagePinDialog.title: Image Pin
imagePinDialog.description: Upload an image, draw regions, and set a question for each area.

imagePinEditor.intro: Upload an image and mark the relevant areas with rectangles. Write a question for each rectangle and set scoring in the node settings. Save, version, and publish the game when you are ready.
imagePinEditor.drawHint: Drag on the image to draw a rectangle.
imagePinEditor.galleryHint: Pick a thumbnail from this game or from images already in your cloud folder.
imagePinEditor.galleryEmpty: No images yet. Upload one above, or add an image to another Image Pin node on this canvas—then they will show up here.
imagePinEditor.helpDefinition:
1. Upload an image.
2. Draw a rectangle around each important part.
3. Write one question for each rectangle.
4. Save the node.
5. Publish the game when you are ready.

Learners will see the image and answer the question for each marked area.
imagePinEditor.helpExampleValues: Upload an anatomy image, draw a rectangle around the heart, and ask: “What is this part called?”
imagePinEditor.questionPlaceholder: What should the learner answer for this region?
imagePinEditor.toastUploadRequiresContext: Sign in with an institution to upload images.
imagePinEditor.toastUploadInvalidType: Please choose a JPEG or PNG image.
imagePinEditor.toastUploadFailed: Could not upload the image. Try again.

imagePinGamePreview.previewNotice: Preview mode. This shows how the game looks and works while you build it.
imagePinGamePreview.submitAnswerPrompt: I placed the pin on the image. Please check my answer and tell me if it is correct.
imagePinGamePreview.hintPrompt: Give me a helpful hint for where I should place the pin, but do not reveal the full answer.
imagePinGamePreview.howToPlayPrompt: Explain how to play this game. Tell me how to answer the question by dragging the pin to the correct place on the image.
imagePinGamePreview.howToPlayScoringResponse:
Scoring:
1. A correct answer on the first try gives {{pointsPerQuestion}} points.
2. Each retry deducts {{deductionPercent}}% from that question's points.
3. Attempt {{maxAttempts}} always gives 0 points, even if the placement is correct.
imagePinGamePreview.howToPlayResponse:
How to play:
1. Read the question above the image.
2. Drag the pin from the slot below onto the image.
3. Place the whole pin inside the highlighted rectangle.
4. Click Submit answer to lock your placement.
5. Correct answers turn the pin blue and show confetti. Wrong answers turn it red.
6. The next question appears after a short pause.

Tip: you can drag the pin back to the slot before you submit.

imagePinSettings.learningFieldLabel: Select the learning field addressed by this node
imagePinSettings.learningFieldPopoverDescription: Select one or more learning fields.
imagePinSettings.gameDescriptionPlaceholder: Describe what this game is about
imagePinSettings.gameDescriptionHint: Learners see this as the first message when they start playing.
imagePinSettings.maxPointsLabel: Maximum score for a fully correct run
imagePinSettings.scoreAccordionHint: Your maximum score is split evenly across the filled questions. The per-question value is floored so the total stays stable.
imagePinSettings.scoreAccordionEmpty: Add at least one non-empty question to calculate the split.
imagePinSettings.retryDeductionAccordionTitle: How is the retry percentage deduction calculated?
imagePinSettings.retryDeductionHint: For one question worth {{pointsPerQuestion}} points, each retry removes {{retryDeductionPercent}}%. Values are floored, and the 4th attempt is automatically 0.
imagePinSettings.retryDeductionEmpty: Add at least one non-empty question to preview retry scoring.
imagePinSettings.timeLimitLabel: Enable optional time limit to challenge advanced players

openQuestionEditor.descriptionPlaceholder: Describe the question, context, and what learners should answer…
openQuestionEditor.exercisesTitle: Add up to 4 exercises. Each one has a learner question and a reference answer used for automatic scoring.
openQuestionEditor.answerPlaceholder: Reference answer used for automatic scoring…

openQuestionDialog.title: Open question
openQuestionDialog.description: Configure the open-ended question node for your learning flow.

openQuestionGamePreview.previewNotice: Preview mode. This shows how the game looks and works while you build it.
openQuestionGamePreview.submitAnswerPrompt: I finished my answer. Please check it.
openQuestionGamePreview.hintPrompt: Give me a helpful hint, but do not reveal the full answer.
openQuestionGamePreview.howToPlayPrompt: Explain how to play this open question.
openQuestionGamePreview.howToPlayResponse:
How to play:
1. Read the question shown in the chat.
2. Type your answer in the text field below.
3. Click Submit answer when you are ready.

Tip: you can still edit your answer until you submit.
openQuestionGamePreview.scoringFailed: We could not score your answer. Please try again.
openQuestionGamePreview.scoringMissingSolution: Add an exercise description in the editor before scoring.
openQuestionGamePreview.scoringMissingReferenceAnswer: Add a reference answer under “Reference answer” for this exercise in the editor.
openQuestionGamePreview.scoringNeedsAttention: This answer may need a teacher review.
openQuestionGamePreview.scoringComingSoon: Automatic scoring is not live in production yet — we are building the PSCA algorithm and it will be available soon 🔬 Your answer was saved in this preview only.
openQuestionGamePreview.iterationFinalSummaryComingSoon: All done! Automatic PSCA scoring is coming to production soon — we are actively working on it 🔬
openQuestionGamePreview.iterationFinalSummary: All done! You earned {{earned}} out of {{total}} points across all questions.
openQuestionGamePreview.submitConfirmDialogBody: If you submit now, your answer can no longer be changed. It will be graded against the reference answer for this question.

openQuestionSettings.maxPointsLabel: Maximum score for a fully correct answer
openQuestionSettings.scoreAccordionHint: The breakdown below shows how the maximum score is distributed once evaluation rules are configured.
openQuestionSettings.scoreAccordionPending: Scoring logic is not configured yet. The current maximum is {{maxPoints}} points — detailed breakdown will appear here after you wire answer evaluation.
openQuestionSettings.scoreAccordionBulletPerQuestion: Total {{maxPoints}} pts · {{questionCount}} exercises · {{pointsPerQuestion}} pts per exercise.
